title: Urban Micro-Apartment Planner with Chess Courtyard
description: Users design a micro-apartment complex within a virtual city by placing and configuring bedroom modules onto building rooftops, while arranging a chess-themed courtyard garden at ground level. A fixed renovation budget acts as a conservation law — upgrading a bedroom module's finish costs points that must be reclaimed by downgrading another module or simplifying the courtyard, with full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load VirtualCity.glb as the base cityscape centered at origin scaled to fit a 20-unit bounding box. Load minimalistic_modern_bedroom.glb as a clonable bedroom module scaled to roughly 1/8 of a city building. Load ABeautifulGame.glb (chess set) scaled small as a decorative courtyard element placeable at street level.

Implementation requirements:
1. BUDGET SYSTEM (conservation law): Display a budget counter starting at 100 points. Placing a bedroom costs 12 points. Placing a chess courtyard costs 8 points. Upgrading a bedroom finish (cycling through 3 tiers via click: basic/wood/marble shown by material color tint white/brown/cream) costs 5 per tier increase and refunds 5 per tier decrease. Total spent must never exceed 100.
2. PLACEMENT: A toolbar with 3 buttons — Add Bedroom, Add Courtyard, Undo, Redo. In Add Bedroom mode, clicking a raycasted point on any building rooftop (top faces, y > threshold) clones and places a bedroom there. In Add Courtyard mode, clicking ground level (y near 0) places a chess set instance.
3. MULTI-INPUT COUPLING: Right-click-drag on any placed bedroom rotates it on Y-axis. Scroll wheel over a placed bedroom scales it between 0.8x and 1.2x (costs/refunds 2 points per scale step, 3 steps each direction). Clicking a placed object selects it (outline highlight via emissive), then clicking upgrade button cycles its tier.
4. UNDO/REDO: Maintain a command stack supporting full undo/redo of place, remove, rotate, scale, and upgrade actions, restoring budget correctly.
5. SCENE: Ambient light 0.5 intensity, directional light from upper-right casting soft shadows. Camera starts at 45-degree elevated angle. OrbitControls enabled. Sky background gradient. HUD overlay shows budget, object count, and action buttons styled with semi-transparent dark panels.
6. DELETE: Pressing Delete key removes selected object, refunding its total cost including upgrades.

Target 10-14 interaction steps for a typical session: place 3-4 bedrooms, place 1-2 courtyards, upgrade/rotate/scale a few, undo a mistake, redo it back.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  budgetRemaining: number,         // points left from initial 100 budget
  bedroomCount: number,         // total bedroom modules placed on rooftops
  courtyardCount: number,         // total chess courtyard elements placed at ground level
  totalSpent: number,         // cumulative budget points spent on placements and upgrades
  averageBedroomTier: number,         // mean finish tier across all bedrooms (0=basic, 1=wood, 2=marble)
  undoStackSize: number,         // count of actions available to undo
  redoStackSize: number,         // count of actions available to redo
  placementMode: string,         // current toolbar mode (idle|bedroom|courtyard)
  lastPlacedObjectType: string,         // type of most recently placed object (bedroom|courtyard|none)
  highestBedroomY: number,         // maximum y-coordinate of any placed bedroom module
  isRotating: boolean,         // whether user is currently right-click-dragging to rotate a bedroom
}
